title: Persistent JIT cache (warm-worker mode productized)
goal: Productize the existing `eshkol-repl --machine` warm-worker mode into a persistent JIT cache so `eshkol-run -r` cold-start drops from ~2s to sub-100ms. Cache stdlib.bc + compiled-form artifacts keyed by content hash of the input source + every stdlib module + LLVM version.
next_actions: lib/repl/repl_cache.cpp: cache key = SHA-256(input.esk + every (require …) chain + stdlib.bc mtime + LLVM version + Eshkol git hash).
On eshkol-run -r: if cache hit at $HOME/.cache/eshkol/jit/<key>.o exists, dlopen it and skip the full JIT compile.
On cache miss: full JIT, then write the resulting object to the cache before exec.
GC: cache entries older than 30 days OR cache size > 1 GiB get evicted on the next miss (LRU).
tests/v1_3_edge_cases/jit_cache_test.sh: time the second invocation of the same file; assert it's at least 5x faster than the first.
acceptance: Second invocation of an identical .esk file takes < 200ms wall-clock.
Source edit invalidates the cache (key includes content hash).
stdlib edit invalidates all entries that transitively depend on it.
Cache size respects the GiB ceiling.
ux_payoff: REPL feels instantaneous instead of like a small wait. Iteration speed for agent code (which uses -r heavily) goes up 5-10x.
